editor_settings.title: Canon G7 X Mark III vs Mark II: Which One Should You Actually Buy?
editor_settings.content:
Canon G7 X Mark III vs Mark II: Which One Should You Actually Buy?

This comparison looks harder than it really is.

If your main goal is video, vlogging, or getting a newer compact that feels more creator-ready, buy the Canon G7 X Mark III. If your goal is saving money and mostly taking photos, the Canon G7 X Mark II is still the smarter value play.

That is the whole story in one paragraph. Everything else is just explaining why.

What the Mark III Does Better

The Mark III is the more modern camera, even if it is not exactly new anymore.

Canon built in the stuff creator buyers care about most:

  • 4K video
  • a 3.5mm microphone input
  • vertical video support
  • live streaming features
  • updated processing around video workflow

That mic jack alone changes the conversation. A lot of compact cameras look cute on paper, then become annoying the second you care about audio.

The Mark III at least gives you a real path to cleaner sound.

What the Mark II Still Gets Right

The Mark II is older, but it did not suddenly become bad because the Mark III exists.

For still photography, the difference is less dramatic than a lot of buyers expect. If you mostly want a small camera for travel, flash shots, casual portraits, and everyday content, the Mark II still delivers the classic G7 X feel.

And if the price gap is meaningful, that matters more than tiny upgrade obsession.

Where the Mark III Is Overrated

Here is the part people need to hear.

The Mark III is not a total reinvention. It is better, yes. But if you only shoot photos and never plug in a mic, the upgrade can feel smaller than the resale market makes it sound.

That is why some buyers overpay for the Mark III when the Mark II would have made them just as happy.

Where the Mark II Feels Old

The second you care about creator workflow, the Mark II gets harder to defend.

No mic input is the biggest miss. If you want to vlog seriously, record cleaner voice, or build a more flexible social setup, the Mark III wins and it is not close.

That is why most video-first buyers should stop trying to save every last dollar and just buy the better tool.

Which One Should You Buy?

Buy the Mark III if you want:

  • 4K video
  • external mic support
  • better creator features
  • a camera that feels more current for video work

Buy the Mark II if you want:

  • the cheaper entry point
  • mostly photos
  • the G7 X look without paying Mark III scarcity pricing

Current Buying Angle

If you are picking between real listings right now, these are the two kinds of offers that make sense to compare:

[product-card:canon-powershot-g7-x-mark-iii-201mp-42x-zoom-black-vlogging-camera-with-wi-fi-touchscreen]


[product-card:canon-powershot-g7-x-mark-ii-201mp-digital-camera-7x-zoom-95-new]


The Mark III is the right choice if you will actually use the added video features. The Mark II is the better deal if you will not.

Final Verdict

For most 2026 buyers, the Canon G7 X Mark III is the better camera. It is the one to get if you care about vlogging, audio, and a more modern compact workflow.

But the Canon G7 X Mark II is still the better value buy for people who mainly want photos and do not need creator extras.

So the best choice is not about which model is newer. It is about whether you are paying for features you will really use.
